summary: `for` loops iterate over any sequence/iterable object. Use `enumerate()` for index+item, `zip()` for parallel sequences.
oneLineSummary: for loops iterate sequentially over items in any iterable object.
keyTakeaways: Never mutate list size during iteration.

memoryTricks.trick: FOR each item IN list, DO action.

commonErrors.cause: Manual index looping `range(len(lst))` out of bounds
commonErrors.fix: Iterate directly over elements `for item in lst`

nextTopics.title: Loop Control Statements
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn break, continue, and pass to control loop behavior.
